content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660               322-5901              322-6441              322-6444

February 28, 1985


John R. Harper
Nazarek, Harper, Hopkins
& McFarlin
2171 Campus Drive, Suite 330
Irvine, CA 92715

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-85-014

Dear Mr. Harper:

Thank you for your letter requesting advice on behalf of
Placentia City Councilmember Robert W. Kuznik regarding his
duties under the conflict of interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act.1/

FACTS

Councilmember Kuznik is President of Jaycox Disposal
Company which provides residential, commercial, and industrial
waste collection services to the City of Placentia, as well as
to other neighboring communities. Councilmember Kuznik does not
have any investment in Jaycox, but he receives a salary for his
services as president.

Councilmember Kuznik has recently formed a company which
will provide computer services and market computer software.
Councilmember Kuznik is the principal stockholder of this
company. He intends that this company will provide data
processing services to Jaycox with regard to Jaycox's processing
of its residential, commercial, and industrial accounts.

___________

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise
indicated.

John R. Harper
February 28, 1985
Page 2


You have stated that Councilmember Kuznik has disqualified
himself from participating in any decisions of the Placentia
City Council related to Jaycox or its contract with the City.

QUESTION

What are Councilmember Kuznik's duties under the conflict
of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act as a result
of his ownership interest in the new computer services company?

CONCLUSION

If it is reasonably foreseeable that a matter before the
City Council would have a material financial effect on the
computer services company, Councilmember Kuznik is required to
disqualify himself from participating in the decision. He must
disclose that he has a conflict of interest, and that disclosure
must be made part of the City Council's official record. These
duties are in addition to any disqualification and disclosure
required under the Political Reform Act when Councilmember
Kuznik has a conflict of interest regarding Jaycox Disposal
Company.

DISCUSSION

Section 87100 prohibits a public official from making or
participating in a governmental decision in which he knows or
has reason to know he has a financial interest.

Section 87103 provides that an official has a financial
interest in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public
generally, on:

(a) Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth one
thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.

(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution
in the regular course of business on terms available
to the public without regard to official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more
in value provided to, received by or promised to the

John R. Harper
February 28, 1985
Page 3


public official within 12 months prior to the time
when the decision is made.

     (d)  Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of management.

     (e)  Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent
for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating two
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided
to, received by, or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision
is made.

                                        Section 87103.

     If Councilmember Kuznik's investment in the computer
services company exceeds $1,000, or if he has received $250 or
more in income from the company during the last 12 months, or if
he holds a position such as director, officer, or employee of
the company.  In addition, if he holds more than a 10 percent
interest in the company, he must not participate in any decision
which could materially affect any investments or any interests
in real property held by the corporation or any sources of
income to the corporation.2/

     For purposes of determining whether the effect of a
decision on the computer services company would be considered
material, you should refer to Commission regulation 2 Cal. Adm.
Code Section 18702 (copy enclosed).

     When Councilmember Kuznik determines that it is reasonably
foreseeable that a decision before the City Council could have a
material financial effect on the computer services company, that
determination must be accompanied by the disclosure of his
interest, and the disclosure must be made part of the agency's
official record (2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18700(b)(5)).

_________________________
     2/  The income of an individual is defined to include a
pro rata share of any income of a business entity in which the
individual has a 10 percent or greater interest.  Section
82030(a).  For example, if he owns a 50 percent interest in the
computer services company, any client of the company, including
Jaycox Disposal Company, who has paid the company $500 or more
during the previous 12 months would be considered a source of
income to him.

John R. Harper
February 28, 1985
Page 4


     You have indicated that Councilmember Kuznik is aware of
his duties under the Political Reform Act regarding his
financial interest in Jaycox Disposal Company; therefore, I have
limited this advice to potential conflicts of interest regarding
the computer services company. With regard to each matter
before the City Council, Councilmember Kuznik must determine
whether he has a conflict of interest as to Jaycox Disposal
Company, the computer services company, or both companies. In
each instance that a conflict of interest exists, Councilmember
Kuznik must disqualify himself from participating in the
decision of the City Council.

     This advice is limited to the provisions of the Political
Reform Act. I have not discussed any issues relating to Section
1090 because the interpretation and enforcement of that section
is outside of the Commission's jurisdiction.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at (916) 322-5901.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [Signature]
                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        Counsel
                                        Legal Division

KED:plh
Enclosure

LAW OFFICES OF

NAZAREK, HARPER, HOPKINS & McFARLIN
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS

EUGENE NAZAREK                                    2171 CAMPUS DRIVE, SUITE 330                348 W. HOSPITALITY LANE
JOHN M. HARPER       [illegible] O [illegible] AF UU        IRVINE, CALIFORNIA 92715                   SUITE 102
IVAN L. HOPKINS                                  (714) 752-8494                         SAN BERNARDINO, CALIFORNIA 92408
GEORGE W. McFARLIN                                                                      (714) 824-8360

*A PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION

BRET H. REED, JR.
OF COUNSEL


January 23, 1985


Janis McLean
Staff Attorney
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Building
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Ms. McLean:

     As City Attorney for the City of Placentia, I am writing this
letter on behalf of Councilmember Robert W. Kuznik, requesting a
formal opinion concerning any potential conflict of interest he
may have, and the disclosure requirements related thereto, based
upon the following factual circumstances.

     Mr. Kuznik is President of Jaycox Disposal Company which provides
residential, commercial and industrial waste collection services
to the City of Placentia, as well as to other neighboring
communities.  Jaycox has provided those services to Placentia for
the last twenty plus years.  Long before Mr. Kuznik became
involved with the Company, Jaycox provided services under a
continuing five-year contract.  If the City determines to cancel a
contract, it must give Jaycox, in essence, four years notice.
This arrangement predates substantially Mr. Kuznik's tenure on the
Council.  Mr. Kuznik does not have any ownership in the company.

     Mr. Kuznik is aware of his conflict with regard to Jaycox and has
absented himself from any discussion related to Jaycox or its
contract with the City and has not participated in any voting
related thereto.  In connection with that abstention, Mr. Kuznik
has disclosed the nature of his interest as required by law.

LAW OFFICES OF
NAZAREK, HARPER, HOPKINS & McFARLIN
A PARTNERSHIP INCLUDING PROFESSIONAL CORPORATIONS


Janis McLean
January 23, 1985
Page Two


Outside of his employment with Jaycox, Mr. Kuznik has recently
formed a company which would provide computer services and market
computer software. He intends that this new entity will
provide data processing services to Jaycox Disposal Company
related to Jaycox processing of its residential, commercial and
industrial accounts. Mr. Kuznik will be the principal stockholder
in this company.

Based upon the foregoing, does Mr. Kuznik have any additional
conflict of interest with regard to this new company and what are
his disclosure requirements.

If you have any questions, please don't hesitate to give me a call
or call Mr. Kuznik directly at 714-634-4551.

Yours truly,

John R. Harper
City Attorney

JRH:gr
cc: Robert Kuznik

Company.
